hawkish_phrases: domestic cost pressures had been increasing
labour cost pressures had strengthened and broadened amid high levels of capacity utilisation and tightening labour markets
negotiated wage growth had risen to 2.2% in the fourth quarter of 2018
wage pressures would eventually pass through to underlying inflation
the risk of an unanchoring of inflation expectations was assessed as very low
dovish_phrases: weakening in economic data pointed to a sizeable moderation in the pace of the economic expansion that would extend into the current year
the weaker economic momentum was slowing the adjustment of inflation towards the Governing Council's aim
outlook for HICP inflation had been revised down significantly by 0.3 percentage points in 2019 and by 0.2 percentage points in both 2020 and 2021
measures of underlying inflation remained muted
longer-term market-based inflation expectations, as derived from the five-year inflation-linked swap rates five years forward, had declined to 1.5%
summary: The ECB held rates unchanged but extended forward guidance on rates remaining at present levels from mid-2019 to at least through end-2019, launched a new TLTRO-III program, and significantly revised down growth and inflation projections in response to weakening economic data and slowing inflation adjustment.
